trans-unit.source: GIS in Anticipatory Action
trans-unit.target: GIS in Anticipatory Action

trans-unit.source: Why GIS for Anticipatory Action?
trans-unit.target: Why GIS for Anticipatory Action?

trans-unit.source: Anticipatory Action (AA) seeks to reduce humanitarian impacts by taking action before a hazard escalates into a disaster. Geographic Information Systems (GIS) support this approach by enabling spatial risk analysis, monitoring triggers and thresholds, estimating potential impacts, and identifying where early actions should be targeted. By integrating these capabilities, GIS helps humanitarian actors make timely, evidence-based decisions. This section of the IFRC GIS Training Platform highlights practical GIS workflows for Anticipatory Action, grounded in real-world humanitarian operations.
trans-unit.target: Anticipatory Action (AA) seeks to reduce humanitarian impacts by taking action before a hazard escalates into a disaster. Geographic Information Systems (GIS) support this approach by enabling spatial risk analysis, monitoring triggers and thresholds, estimating potential impacts, and identifying where early actions should be targeted. By integrating these capabilities, GIS helps humanitarian actors make timely, evidence-based decisions. This section of the IFRC GIS Training Platform highlights practical GIS workflows for Anticipatory Action, grounded in real-world humanitarian operations.

trans-unit.source: What You’ll Find in This Section
trans-unit.target: What You’ll Find in This Section

trans-unit.source: The GIS in Anticipatory Action content brings together **case studies, applied analyses, and exercises** that demonstrate how GIS supports AA across different hazards and countries.
trans-unit.target: The GIS in Anticipatory Action content brings together **case studies, applied analyses, and exercises** that demonstrate how GIS supports AA across different hazards and countries.

trans-unit.source: You will find:
trans-unit.target: You will find:

trans-unit.source: Country- and hazard-specific case studies
trans-unit.target: Country- and hazard-specific case studies

trans-unit.source: End-to-end GIS workflows
trans-unit.target: End-to-end GIS workflows

trans-unit.source: Examples of trigger and impact analyses
trans-unit.target: Examples of trigger and impact analyses

trans-unit.source: Exercises focused on AA-relevant methods
trans-unit.target: Exercises focused on AA-relevant methods

trans-unit.source: This content is designed for:
trans-unit.target: This content is designed for:

trans-unit.source: GIS trainers
trans-unit.target: GIS trainers

trans-unit.source: AA practitioners
trans-unit.target: AA practitioners

trans-unit.source: Technical staff supporting anticipatory decision-making
trans-unit.target: Technical staff supporting anticipatory decision-making

trans-unit.source: Exercises: GIS for Anticipatory Action
trans-unit.target: Exercises: GIS for Anticipatory Action

trans-unit.source: In addition to the case studies, this section includes hands-on follow-along exercises that focus on the specific GIS techniques used in the case studies:
trans-unit.target: In addition to the case studies, this section includes hands-on follow-along exercises that focus on the specific GIS techniques used in the case studies:

trans-unit.target: __Cyclone Anticipatory Action Analysis Exercise Track__

trans-unit.target: Spatial risk analysis

trans-unit.target: Exposure and impact estimation

trans-unit.target: Working with hazard and vulnerability data

trans-unit.source: These exercises can be used independently or embedded into broader AA-focused trainings.
trans-unit.target: These exercises can be used independently or embedded into broader AA-focused trainings.

trans-unit.source: Tools and Methods Used in GIS for AA
trans-unit.target: Tools and Methods Used in GIS for AA

trans-unit.source: Many GIS analyses in Anticipatory Action analyses rely on **specialised tools and platforms**. These tools are documented in the **Tools & Methods** section of this platform.
trans-unit.target: Many GIS analyses in Anticipatory Action analyses rely on **specialised tools and platforms**. These tools are documented in the **Tools & Methods** section of this platform.

trans-unit.source: Commonly used tools include:
trans-unit.target: Commonly used tools include:

trans-unit.source: Risk Assessment QGIS Plugin
trans-unit.target: Risk Assessment QGIS Plugin

trans-unit.source: AILAS
trans-unit.target: AILAS

trans-unit.source: GAIA
trans-unit.target: GAIA

trans-unit.source: Each tool is documented separately to highlight:
trans-unit.target: Each tool is documented separately to highlight:

trans-unit.source: Reusability beyond a single case study
trans-unit.target: Reusability beyond a single case study

trans-unit.source: Application across different hazards and countries
trans-unit.target: Application across different hazards and countries

trans-unit.source: 👉 Refer to the **Tools & Methods** section for detailed documentation.
trans-unit.target: 👉 Refer to the **Tools & Methods** section for detailed documentation.

trans-unit.source: Getting Started
trans-unit.target: Getting Started

trans-unit.source: If you are new to GIS for Anticipatory Action:
trans-unit.target: If you are new to GIS for Anticipatory Action:

trans-unit.source: Start with the **basic GIS modules**
trans-unit.target: Start with the **basic GIS modules**

trans-unit.source: Explore one **AA case study**
trans-unit.target: Explore one **AA case study**

trans-unit.source: Review the **tools** used in that case
trans-unit.target: Review the **tools** used in that case

trans-unit.source: Integrate selected exercises into your training or workflow
trans-unit.target: Integrate selected exercises into your training or workflow
